behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur at module boundaries: CSV row indexing, schema validation, and DLQ serialization/routing. A pure unit test can miss off-by-one row-number mapping or dropped metadata when components are wired together.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against a small CSV fixture containing valid and invalid rows using real parser + validation + DLQ adapter (backed by local filesystem table or test queue). Assert invalid rows are absent from normalized output, present in DLQ, and include exact original CSV row numbers.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic parsing/normalization logic in a local transformation boundary with no required I/O. Real failures are usually format-handling bugs in the conversion function itself.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including ambiguous dates and timezone-bearing values), asserting exact canonical output or expected rejection for invalid/ambiguous cases.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Timeout and memory behavior depend on runtime/OS characteristics and streaming behavior under realistic load. In-process mocks generally hide true memory pressure and throughput bottlenecks.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline process end-to-end on a generated 500k-row CSV with production-like configuration. Measure wall-clock completion against configured timeout and monitor peak RSS/heap usage to ensure no OOM or runaway memory growth.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real HTTP error handling and retry timing behavior across client stack layers. A pure mock can falsely pass while missing status-code mapping, retry middleware, or backoff scheduling defects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against an HTTP endpoint or S3-compatible test server that returns 503 for initial requests and then succeeds. Verify retry attempts, increasing backoff intervals, and eventual batch success without duplicate side effects.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary includes crash/restart semantics plus durable DB state and idempotency guarantees. Mocked restarts or mocked DB writes can hide transaction-commit and resume-offset bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Start processing a fixture batch against a real PostgreSQL instance, force-stop the pipeline mid-batch after a known checkpoint, then restart. Assert final row count and unique keys match exactly-once expectations with no duplicates for previously committed rows.
